fieldValues.value: Add requiresRelation to LifecycleState and fulfillment to the transition operation

lifecycle.json ($defs.LifecycleState + $defs.RequiresRelation), type.json (inline LifecycleState copy), ext:lifecycle extension text, record transition CLI contract, allowed-transitions projection, repo validate

LifecycleState gains optional requiresRelation {relationType: string|string[] (required, any-of, no implicit default), direction: incoming|outgoing (default incoming)}. A Record may only occupy such a state when a satisfying Relation exists; transitions in are hard-rejected unless already satisfied or fulfilled atomically via the transition operation input fulfillment.newRecord / fulfillment.existingInstanceId (with optional relationType selector). Allowed-transitions options carry the target state declaration; validation emits warning-severity LIFECYCLE_RELATION_UNSATISFIED for at-rest violations. Conformance rules R1-R10; invariants I-88/I-89/I-90.

The obligation lives on the state, not the transition edge: every path into the state inherits it (no door-dependent integrity) and at-rest orphan detection falls out of the same declared invariant. Extending the single transition write path avoids a verb per relation-obligation. Hard rejection is the load-bearing choice — advisory enforcement is exactly the per-client discipline whose failure produced orphan supersessions (srs-web#204).
